On April 13, 2025, the National Communications Authority (NCA) of Somalia officially awarded an operating license to Starlink, and Elon Musk announced on X that Starlink is 'now available' in Somalia. Reports from May 5, 2025, confirmed that Starlink's publicly available service maps have officially been updated to reflect coverage in Somalia and that users can order kits. Because the service officially launched and the map appears to have already been updated prior to the May 2025 knowledge cutoff, it is virtually certain that the Starlink Availability Map will show Somalia as 'Available' before the July 1, 2025 deadline. The probability is set to the maximum allowable boundary of 0.95 given this strong, definitive evidence.

The fast pace of direct negotiations between the US and Iran (four rounds within one month) strongly suggests a concentrated effort to reach an agreement. Israeli senior ministers being briefed that there is a 'high chance' of a deal further supports the likelihood of a breakthrough. With Trump's reported two-month deadline arriving in mid-May, the tight six-week window before the July 1, 2025 deadline will likely see the climax of these talks. Furthermore, the resolution criteria specify 'an agreement,' which would encompass an interim deal, a joint framework, or a freeze-for-freeze — all of which are significantly easier to conclude than a comprehensive treaty. Trump's history with North Korea demonstrates he is capable of moving quickly from threats to signing a high-level (even if less substantive) agreement. However, significant substantive differences remain (e.g., the US demanding total elimination of enrichment versus Iran proposing kept enrichment via a joint venture). Also, talks have experienced friction and delays due to new sanctions. Consequently, while momentum points heavily toward an announcement that halts the current escalation cycle, structural obstacles and the possibility of talks dragging out slightly past July keep the probability balanced around 55%.

Chad's official operating license for Starlink was formally signed on March 13, 2025. The deadline to be listed as 'Available' is July 1, 2025, exactly 110 days (about 3.6 months) after signing. Recent Starlink launches in African nations (such as Zimbabwe, Zambia, Ghana, and Niger) have typically taken 3 to 4 months between formal license agreement and actual launch. While Elon Musk's premature announcement of Chad's availability in November 2024 suggests high executive priority and potential pre-staging of operations, Chad is deeply landlocked with complex logistics and bureaucratic hurdles that reliably delay execution. Furthermore, as of the May 15 cutoff, 63 days have passed with no launch. With the deadline arriving right at the statistical mode for anticipated delays, a launch could reasonably happen in June or slip into July. Given the country's challenging environment, the chance of executing the launch perfectly before the July 1 deadline is less than 50%, balanced slightly by the corporate incentive to wrap up objectives by the June 30 (Q2) date. Therefore, a probability of ~32% is well-calibrated.
